title: Batch Processing with root_depth_metrics()
text: folder segmented minirhizotron images want depth-resolved root traits one call — without writing loops — root_depth_metrics() right starting point. handles file loading, depth-map construction, per-bin zonal statistics, derived metrics, fault tolerance, optional file output. toggle metric groups compute; everything else taken care . line--line explanation underlying steps, see Minirhizotron Scans vignette.

title: Batch Processing with root_depth_metrics()
text: Images three directories must alphabetical order ; position corresponds physical tube/window.

title: Batch Processing with root_depth_metrics()
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("jcunow/RootScanR")  library(RootScanR) library(tidyverse)   # for downstream plotting

title: Batch Processing with root_depth_metrics()
text: fastest way get going. path.seg path.skl required; everything else sensible defaults. result data frame one row per tube × depth-bin combination. Always-present columns: Tube, Session, Plot, depth. Default metric columns: rootpx, voidpx, rootlength, avg.diameter, max.diameter, var.diameter, rootpx.density, rootlength.density, rwdi, rpi, total.length.density, rootlength.fraction, mean.var.diameter, rootsurface_rootvolume_ratio.
code: result <- root_depth_metrics(   path.seg = "scans/segmented/2022_02/",   path.skl = "scans/skeleton/2022_02/",   session  = "2022_02" )  head(result)

title: Batch Processing with root_depth_metrics()
text: tubes differ insertion angle soil-surface position, supply vector values (one per image). accept single recycled value images share setting. tube names follow different convention default (characters 3–5 right filename), always supply tube_names explicitly.
code: # Load your field metadata table tube_meta <- read.csv("metadata/tube_metadata_2022_02.csv") # Expected columns: filename, angle, soil_row, tube_id  result <- root_depth_metrics(   path.seg         = "scans/segmented/2022_02/",   path.skl         = "scans/skeleton/2022_02/",   insertion_angles = tube_meta$angle,       # degrees from vertical   soil_starts      = tube_meta$soil_row,    # pixel row of soil surface   tube_names       = tube_meta$tube_id,     # e.g. "T042"   session          = "2022_02",   dpi              = 300 )

title: Batch Processing with root_depth_metrics()
text: metric group logical argument. Disabled groups produce NA columns rather absent columns, downstream code can always reference column names regardless computed.
code: result <- root_depth_metrics(   path.seg  = "scans/segmented/2022_02/",   path.skl  = "scans/skeleton/2022_02/",   path.rgb  = "scans/blended/2022_02/",   session   = "2022_02",    # --- core (on by default, shown explicitly for clarity) ---   calc_root_pixels    = TRUE,   calc_root_length    = TRUE,   calc_diameter_stats = TRUE,    # --- derived (on by default) ---   calc_density_metrics      = TRUE,   calc_distribution_indices = TRUE,   calc_advanced_metrics     = TRUE,    # --- extended (off by default — enable as needed) ---   calc_diameter_quantiles = TRUE,   # 90th/95th/99th percentile + modal peaks   calc_color_metrics      = TRUE,   # RGB colour per root vs background pixels   calc_root_angles        = TRUE,   # deep_drive + steepness angle distribution   calc_landscape_metrics  = FALSE   # slow — enable only when needed )

title: Batch Processing with root_depth_metrics()
text: calc_diameter_quantiles = TRUE, can specify custom diameter thresholds separate fine roots coarse roots:
code: result <- root_depth_metrics(   path.seg                = "scans/segmented/2022_02/",   path.skl                = "scans/skeleton/2022_02/",   calc_diameter_quantiles = TRUE,   diameter_thresholds     = c(0.2, 0.5, 1.0),   # fine / medium / coarse   diameter_threshold_unit = "mm"                 # "mm", "cm", or "px" ) # Resulting columns include: rootlength.above.0.2mm, rootlength.above.0.5mm, ...

title: Batch Processing with root_depth_metrics()
text: glass-fronted rhizotron boxes (flat geometry, tube curvature), set flat_geometry = TRUE. disables sinusoidal depth correction otherwise applied cylindrical minirhizotron tubes.
code: result <- root_depth_metrics(   path.seg      = "scans/segmented/rhizotron_A/",   path.skl      = "scans/skeleton/rhizotron_A/",   flat_geometry = TRUE,   insertion_angles = 0   # vertical windows )

title: Batch Processing with root_depth_metrics()
text: skeleton RGB directories contain files segmented directory (e.g. multiple sessions one folder), use *_file_index select matching subset:
code: # Use only files 37–72 from the RGB directory result <- root_depth_metrics(   path.seg       = "scans/segmented/2022_02/",   path.skl       = "scans/skeleton/2022_02/",   path.rgb       = "scans/blended/all_sessions/",   rgb_file_index = 37:72,   session        = "2022_02" )

title: Batch Processing with root_depth_metrics()
text: Pass output_path save result .RData file (object named root.depth.metrics inside file). directory created exist.
code: result <- root_depth_metrics(   path.seg    = "scans/segmented/2022_02/",   path.skl    = "scans/skeleton/2022_02/",   session     = "2022_02",   output_path = "output/root_metrics_2022_02.RData" )  # Reload later: # load("output/root_metrics_2022_02.RData") # head(root.depth.metrics)

title: Batch Processing with root_depth_metrics()
text: default (verbose = TRUE) function prints progress line image showing per-image time, cumulative elapsed time, estimated remaining time, predicted clock-time completion: metric block fails single image (e.g. corrupted skeleton file), skipped [RootScanR] SKIPPED message corresponding columns filled NA. Processing always continues. Images loaded skipped entirely listed warning end. suppress progress output:
code: [RootScanR] [3/48] T042 | img: 12s | elapsed: 38s | remaining: ~546s | done ~14:22 result <- root_depth_metrics(   path.seg = "scans/segmented/2022_02/",   path.skl = "scans/skeleton/2022_02/",   verbose  = FALSE )

title: Batch Processing with root_depth_metrics()
code: library(ggplot2)  # Root length density by depth, one line per tube ggplot(result, aes(x = depth, y = rootlength.density, colour = Tube)) +   geom_line() +   geom_point() +   coord_flip() +   scale_x_reverse() +   theme_minimal() +   labs(     title = "Root Length Density by Depth",     x     = "Soil Depth (cm)",     y     = "Root Length Density (cm root / cm² area)"   )  # Average root diameter by depth ggplot(result, aes(x = depth, y = avg.diameter)) +   geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = TRUE) +   coord_flip() +   scale_x_reverse() +   theme_minimal() +   labs(     title = "Average Root Diameter with Depth",     x     = "Soil Depth (cm)",     y     = "Average Diameter (cm)"   )  # RWDI distribution across tubes ggplot(result |> dplyr::distinct(Tube, rwdi), aes(x = rwdi)) +   geom_histogram(bins = 15, fill = "steelblue4", colour = "white") +   theme_minimal() +   labs(     title = "Root Weight Distribution Index (RWDI)",     x     = "RWDI",     y     = "Count"   )

title: Batch Processing with root_depth_metrics()
code: tube_meta <- read.csv("metadata/tube_metadata_2022_02.csv")  result <- root_depth_metrics(    # Paths   path.seg       = "scans/segmented/2022_02/",   path.skl       = "scans/skeleton/2022_02/",   path.rgb       = "scans/blended/2022_02/",    # Per-image metadata   insertion_angles = tube_meta$angle,   soil_starts      = tube_meta$soil_row,   tube_names       = tube_meta$tube_id,   session          = "2022_02",    # Geometry   dpi               = 300,   tube_diameter_cm  = 7,   depth_interval_cm = 5,   flat_geometry     = FALSE,    # Core metrics   calc_root_pixels    = TRUE,   calc_root_length    = TRUE,   calc_diameter_stats = TRUE,    # Extended metrics   calc_diameter_quantiles = TRUE,   calc_color_metrics      = TRUE,   calc_root_angles        = TRUE,   calc_landscape_metrics  = FALSE,   # enable if you need patch metrics    # Derived metrics   calc_density_metrics      = TRUE,   calc_distribution_indices = TRUE,   calc_advanced_metrics     = TRUE,    # Diameter thresholds (for quantile block)   diameter_thresholds     = c(0.2, 0.5, 1.0),   diameter_threshold_unit = "mm",    # Output   output_path = "output/root_metrics_2022_02.RData",   verbose     = TRUE )

title: Analyzing Root Traits from Flatbed Scans with RootScanR in R
text: Flatbed scanning captures root morphology 2D plane high resolution controlled lighting. Unlike minirhizotron images, flatbed scans depth dimension — full root system visible traits computed globally rather per depth bin. vignette covers individual-function workflow flatbed images. working minirhizotron tubes want single-call batch approach, see Batch Processing vignette instead. Prerequisite: RootScanR works already-segmented images (root = 1, background = 0). Segmentation must done beforehand using RootDetector RootPainter.

previous_headings: Analyzing Root Systems from Flatbed Scans
title: Analyzing Root Traits from Flatbed Scans with RootScanR in R
code: # install.packages("remotes") # remotes::install_github("jcunow/RootScanR")  library(RootScanR) library(terra) library(tidyverse)

title: Analyzing Root Traits from Flatbed Scans with RootScanR in R
text: load_flexible_image() accepts rasters, arrays, file paths, common image formats. Set binarize = TRUE force image 0/1 values.
code: # Segmented image (binary: root = 1, background = 0) seg <- load_flexible_image(   "path/to/segmented_scan.tif",   output_format = "spatrast",   normalize     = TRUE,   binarize      = TRUE,   select.layer  = 2        # RootDetector: layer 2 is the root channel )  # Original RGB scan for colour analysis rgb <- load_flexible_image(   "path/to/rgb_scan.tif",   output_format = "spatrast",   normalize     = FALSE )  plot(seg, main = "Segmented scan") plot(rgb, main = "RGB scan")

previous_headings: Analyzing Root Systems from Flatbed Scans > Workflow
title: Analyzing Root Traits from Flatbed Scans with RootScanR in R
text: skeletonize_image() reduces roots single-pixel-wide centrelines. required root length (Kimura method) diameter estimation. Available methods: "ZhangSuen", "GuoHall", "MAT" (Medial Axis Transform), "SteepestAscend". multiple methods supplied results combined.
code: skl <- skeletonize_image(   seg,   methods      = c("ZhangSuen", "GuoHall"),  # ensemble for robustness   select.layer = 2,   verbose      = TRUE )  plot(skl, main = "Skeleton")

previous_headings: Analyzing Root Systems from Flatbed Scans > Workflow
title: Analyzing Root Traits from Flatbed Scans with RootScanR in R
text: root_length() uses Kimura’s formula, weights orthogonal diagonal skeleton segments differently approximate true path length.
code: rl <- root_length(skl, unit = "cm", dpi = 300, select.layer = NULL) cat("Total root length:", round(rl, 2), "cm\n")

previous_headings: Analyzing Root Systems from Flatbed Scans > Workflow
title: Analyzing Root Traits from Flatbed Scans with RootScanR in R
text: root_diameter() estimates local root width distance transform. returns diameter raster raw diameter values. fine/coarse root separation, modal_peaks() can find diameter modes distribution:
code: diam_result <- root_diameter(   seg,   skeleton.img    = skl,   skeleton_method = "MAT",   select.layer    = NULL,   unit            = "cm" )  # Summary statistics diam_vals <- terra::values(diam_result$diameter_rast, na.rm = TRUE) cat(sprintf(   "Diameter — mean: %.3f cm  SD: %.3f cm  max: %.3f cm\n",   mean(diam_vals), sd(diam_vals), max(diam_vals) ))  # Distribution hist(diam_vals,      breaks = 40,      xlab   = "Diameter (cm)",      main   = "Root diameter distribution",      col    = "steelblue4") peaks <- modal_peaks(   diam_vals,   display_type         = "density",   prominence_threshold = length(diam_vals) / sqrt(length(diam_vals)),   mclust               = FALSE ) cat("Diameter peaks at (cm):", paste(round(peaks$peak_x, 3), collapse = ", "), "\n")

text: create_depthmap() assigns soil depth value (cm) every pixel based tube geometry (diameter, tilt angle, DPI) sinusoidal correction curvature cylindrical tubes. Important parameters: start.soil: accurate depth attribution requires knowing soil surface image. -situ calibration (marking tube installation) reliable approach. code: # Use layer 1 as spatial template; actual root layer is separate seg_template <- seg[[1]]  depth_map <- create_depthmap(   img           = seg_template,   sinoid        = TRUE,   tube.thicc    = 7,       # cm — measure your own tube   tilt          = 45,      # degrees   dpi           = 300,   start.soil    = 2.9,     # cm — from in-situ calibration   center.offset = 0.5      # 0 = top of tube, 1 = bottom )  # Align the depth map extent to the segmented image depth_map <- terra::flip(terra::t(depth_map)) terra::ext(depth_map) <- terra::ext(seg_template)  plot(depth_map, main = "Depth map (cm)")

title: Rotation Bias and Rhythmicity Analysis with RootScanR
text: vignette demonstrates detect correct rotational bias minirhizotron setups using RootScanR package. minirhizotron studies, scanner tube inserted angle soil can rotate slightly sessions. CI-600 similar scanners cover full 360° arc, sequential images tube may perfectly overlap. Left uncorrected, rotation introduces systematic spatial bias — roots near scan edges observed sessions others. RootScanR addresses three calibration functions: estimate_rotation_center() — locates rotational zero point tape coverage single image estimate_rotation_shift() — quantifies pixel offset two sessions using image correlation rotation_censor() — crops images shared, overlap region

title: Rotation Bias and Rhythmicity Analysis with RootScanR
code: # install.packages("remotes") # remotes::install_github("jcunow/RootScanR")  library(RootScanR) library(terra)

what: 1. Estimate the rotational center from a single image
title: Rotation Bias and Rhythmicity Analysis with RootScanR
text: estimate_rotation_center() detects white adhesive tape attached upper side tube. tape visible one side, function infers top tube (rotational zero) lies. returns pixel row index. Since tube fixed geometry, needs done per tube — rotational center change sessions. Key parameters:
code: data(seg_Oulanka2023_Session01_T067) img <- terra::rast(seg_Oulanka2023_Session01_T067)  r0 <- estimate_rotation_center(img) print(paste("Estimated rotation center (row):", r0))

what: 2. Estimate the rotation shift between two sessions
title: Rotation Bias and Rhythmicity Analysis with RootScanR
text: tube scanned two different sessions, scanner may rotated. estimate_rotation_shift() uses either cross-correlation ("ccf") phase correlation ("phase") shared depth window find pixel offset. Phase correlation generally robust brightness differences sessions. returned vector c(x.lag, y.lag) — horizontal (depth axis) vertical (rotation axis) pixel shifts. large vertical shift means tube rotated substantially sessions.
code: data(seg_Oulanka2023_Session01_T067) data(seg_Oulanka2023_Session03_T067)  shift <- estimate_rotation_shift(   seg_Oulanka2023_Session01_T067,   seg_Oulanka2023_Session03_T067,   cor.type          = "phase",   fixed.depth.pixel = c(1000, 4000) )  cat("Rotation shift (depth px, rotation px):", shift, "\n")

what: 3. Censor image edges to the shared overlap region
title: Rotation Bias and Rhythmicity Analysis with RootScanR
text: rotation_censor() crops image rows present every session retained. eliminates non-overlapping margins makes root counts directly comparable across sessions. Two modes available: fixed.rotation = FALSE — cuts proportionally based measured offset; output width varies fixed.rotation = TRUE — centres crop specified row forces fixed output width; recommended comparing multiple sessions Note tube geometry. inner outer tube diameters differ, observed root length slightly underestimates true root length soil. resize coefficient may applied separately; rotation_censor() handle correction.
code: data(seg_Oulanka2023_Session01_T067) img <- terra::rast(seg_Oulanka2023_Session01_T067)  # Fixed mode — crop to a 1800-pixel-wide window centred on the rotation center censored <- rotation_censor(   img,   center.offset  = r0,   cut.buffer     = 0.02,   fixed.width    = 1800,   fixed.rotation = TRUE )  terra::plot(censored, main = "Censored image")

title: Rotation Bias and Rhythmicity Analysis with RootScanR
text: deployments, minirhizotron observations made high temporal frequency (e.g. multiple scans per day short window). datasets can useful test whether root observations show rhythmic pattern across sampling cycle. rhythmicity() fits sine curve form y=Asin⁡(2πP(x+ϕ))+cy = \sin\!\left(\frac{2\pi}{P}(x + \phi)\right) + c tests whether amplitude AA significantly different zero, using choice three statistical tests. period choice: period PP reflect biologically methodologically meaningful cycle sampling design — example length monitoring campaign, interval conditions repeat. Use fix_period = NULL genuinely want estimate period data enough observations support four-parameter fit.

title: Rotation Bias and Rhythmicity Analysis with RootScanR
code: set.seed(42) x <- seq(0, 48, length.out = 120) y <- 1.8 * sin(2 * pi / 24 * (x + 5)) + 4 + rnorm(120, 0, 1.2)  # F-test (default) — most interpretable for small samples res_F <- rhythmicity(x, y, fix_period = 24, method = "F") cat(sprintf("Amplitude: %.2f | Phase: %.1f | R²: %.3f | p: %.4f\n",             res_F$amplitude, res_F$phase, res_F$R2, res_F$p_value))  # Finite-sample likelihood ratio test res_FLR <- rhythmicity(x, y, fix_period = 24, method = "FLR")  # Large-sample likelihood ratio test res_LR  <- rhythmicity(x, y, fix_period = 24, method = "LR")

title: Rotation Bias and Rhythmicity Analysis with RootScanR
text: Caution free-period fits. four free parameters likelihood surface can multimodal. Provide starting values grounded sampling design always inspect fitted curve interpreting results.
code: res_free <- rhythmicity(x, y,                         fix_period = NULL,                         parStart   = list(amp = 2, phase = 0,                                           offset = 4, period = 20))  cat(sprintf("Estimated period: %.1f | R²: %.3f | p: %.4f\n",             res_free$period, res_free$R2, res_free$p_value))

title: Rotation Bias and Rhythmicity Analysis with RootScanR
code: x_seq <- seq(min(x), max(x), length.out = 300) y_fit <- res_F$amplitude *          sin(2 * pi / res_F$period * (x_seq + res_F$phase)) +          res_F$offset  plot(x, y, pch = 16, col = "grey50",      xlab = "Observation index", ylab = "Root measurement",      main = "Rhythmicity fit") lines(x_seq, y_fit, col = "coral", lwd = 2) legend("topright",        legend = sprintf("A = %.2f, R² = %.3f, p = %.4f",                         res_F$amplitude, res_F$R2, res_F$p_value),        bty = "n")

title: Rotation Bias and Rhythmicity Analysis with RootScanR
text: significant p-value indicates rhythmic component. Always inspect R2R^2 alongside — significant low-R2R^2 result may simply reflect noisy dataset rather meaningful pattern.

what: Clean a binary root image — clean_image
title: Clean a binary root image — clean_image
text: Performs three sequential cleaning operations binary segmented image: 1. **Hole filling** — fills black regions enclosed white (segmentation    gaps inside roots). 2. **Artifact removal** — removes isolated white specks connected    image border (false-positive root detections). 3. **Edge smoothing** *(optional, default)* — applies morphological    closing smooth jagged root edges.

title: Clean a binary root image — clean_image
code: clean_image(   img,   max_hole_size = NULL,   max_artifact_size = NULL,   edge_smooth = FALSE,   kernel_shape = "disk",   kernel_size = 3,   iterations = 1,   select.layer = NULL,   output_format = "spatrast",   report = FALSE )

title: Clean a binary root image — clean_image
text: img `cimg` object, `SpatRaster`, matrix, file path. max_hole_size Maximum hole size pixels fill.  `NULL`, enclosed holes filled.  See **Choosing thresholds** . max_artifact_size Maximum artifact size pixels remove.  `NULL`, isolated white regions removed. edge_smooth Logical.  Apply morphological closing hole/artifact cleaning.  Default `FALSE`. kernel_shape Structuring element shape edge smoothing: `"disk"` (default), `"square"`, `"diamond"`. kernel_size Structuring element size (odd integer).  Default `3`. iterations Number closing iterations edge smoothing. Default `1`. select.layer Integer `NULL`.  layer use multi-layer inputs. output_format Character.  Format returned object.  One `"spatrast"` (default), `"cimg"`, `"matrix"`.  Using `"spatrast"` means result can passed directly `terra::plot()`, `skeletonize_image()`, `root_length()`, etc. without conversion. report Logical.  `TRUE`, also calls [report_image_components()] *original* (uncleaned) image cleaning.  `output_format = "spatrast"` (default), cleaned raster returned directly even `report = TRUE`; report printed side effect.  Default `FALSE`.

title: Clean a binary root image — clean_image
text: cleaned image format specified `output_format`   (`SpatRaster`, `cimg`, matrix).

title: Clean a binary root image — clean_image
text: `skeletonize_image()` uses Medial Axis Transform, driven distance transform.  Small holes inside root inflate local distance values force medial axis bifurcate around hole, producing spurious branching points.  Isolated artifact pixels produce phantom skeleton segments.  Cleaning first yields much cleaner skeleton.

title: Clean a binary root image — clean_image
text: Call [report_image_components()] image first see actual pixel counts holes artifacts.  300 DPI, sensible starting values `max_hole_size = 50` `max_artifact_size = 10`.

title: Clean a binary root image — clean_image
text: `edge_smooth = TRUE` applies morphological closing slightly dilates erodes root edges.  can merge closely adjacent roots alter root diameter measurements.  use segmentation output jagged edges; leave (`FALSE`, default) otherwise.

title: Clean a binary root image — clean_image
code: data(seg_Oulanka2023_Session01_T067) img <- terra::rast(seg_Oulanka2023_Session01_T067)   # Clean: fill small holes, remove tiny artifacts — returns SpatRaster cleaned <- clean_image(img,                        max_hole_size     = 50,                        max_artifact_size = 10,                        select.layer      = 2)  # If you need a cimg for further imager operations: cleaned_cimg <- clean_image(img, max_hole_size = 50,                             output_format = "cimg", select.layer = 2)

title: Detect and Classify Modes in a Distribution Using Prominence or Mclust — modal_peaks

text: path.seg Character. Path directory binary segmented images (foreground/root pixel = 1, background = 0). path.skl Character NULL. Path directory skeletonised images (one-pixel-wide centrelines roots).  Required length, diameter, angle metrics.  Default NULL. path.rgb Character NULL. Path directory blended RGB images, aligned segmented images.  Required colour metrics. Default NULL. seg_file_index Integer vector NULL. Optional subset index applied list.files(path.seg), e.g. 37:72.  Default NULL (use files). skl_file_index Integer vector NULL. Optional subset index applied list.files(path.skl).  Default NULL. rgb_file_index Integer vector NULL. Optional subset index applied list.files(path.rgb).  Default NULL. insertion_angles Numeric. Insertion angle tube window vertical, degrees.  0 = perfectly vertical, 30 = tilted 30° vertical (common minirhizotron angle). Used create_depthmap() correct depth scale. Default 0. soil_starts Numeric. Pixel row (original, un-rotated image) soil surface begins.  Used set zero-depth reference. Default 0. tube_names Character NULL. Sample/tube identifiers added Tube column.  NULL, names derived characters 3–5 right segmented file name, prefixed "T" (e.g. "T042").  Adjust naming convention differs. Default NULL. session Character. Session campaign label added Session column, e.g. "2022_02".  Default "". dpi Numeric. Scanner resolution dots per inch.  Used convert pixel distances physical units (cm).  Default 300. tube_diameter_cm Numeric. Inner diameter minirhizotron tube centimetres.  Passed create_depthmap() tube.thicc.  role flat-window geometry (flat_geometry = TRUE) uncertain; likely default inside create_depthmap() may ignored — leave default unless know matters setup.  Default 7. depth_interval_cm Numeric. Size depth bin centimetres.  Passed nn binning(). Default 5. flat_geometry Logical.  FALSE (default), images treated cylindrical minirhizotron tubes sinusoidal depth correction applied (sinoid = TRUE create_depthmap()).  Set TRUE flat rhizotron windows (e.g. glass-fronted boxes) sinusoidal correction needed (sinoid = FALSE).  Default FALSE. calc_root_pixels Logical. Count rootpx (foreground pixels) voidpx (background pixels) per depth bin.  Required density metrics.  Default TRUE. calc_root_length Logical. Estimate root length (cm) per depth bin skeleton using D8 connectivity (orthogonal steps = 1 pixel, diagonal steps = \(\sqrt{2}\) pixels, isolated pixels = 1 pixel). Required length density angle metrics.  Default TRUE. calc_diameter_stats Logical. Compute per-bin mean, maximum, variance root diameter (cm) using distance-transform approach root_diameter().  Default TRUE. calc_diameter_quantiles Logical. Compute 90th, 95th, 99th percentile diameter distribution per bin, conditional means quantile, threshold-based root lengths (see diameter_thresholds), modal diameter peaks via modal_peaks().  Default FALSE. calc_landscape_metrics Logical. Compute patch-level landscape metrics per depth bin via root_scape_metrics(): nearest-neighbour distance (enn_mn), joint entropy (joinent), relative mutual information (relmutinf), number patches (np), contagion (contag).  Slow: one call per depth bin per image.  Default FALSE. calc_color_metrics Logical. Compute mean chromatic coordinates (rcc, gcc, bcc), hue, saturation, luminosity, raw RGB channel means separately root pixels background pixels via tube_coloration().  Requires path.rgb.  Default FALSE. calc_root_angles Logical. Compute deep_drive (fraction skeleton pixels whose D8 flow direction matches locally optimal downward direction) mean.steepness.angle / sd.steepness.angle (degrees, 0 = horizontal, 90 = vertical). Uses deep_drive().  Default FALSE. calc_density_metrics Logical. Compute rootpx.density (percent root area cover) rootlength.density (cm root length per cm² imaged area) per bin.  Auto-enables calc_root_pixels calc_root_length.  Default TRUE. calc_distribution_indices Logical. Compute tube-level indices: rwdi (root weight distribution index), rpi (root proliferation index), total.length.density (summed length density bins, cm root per cm² per cm depth).  Auto-enables calc_density_metrics.  Default TRUE. calc_advanced_metrics Logical. Compute per-bin derived metrics: rootlength.fraction (bin's length density fraction tube total), mean.var.diameter (mean within-bin diameter variance), rootsurface_rootvolume_ratio (lateral surface area cylinder volume, summed skeleton pixels bin expressed cm² per cm³).  Auto-enables calc_distribution_indices calc_diameter_stats.  Default TRUE. diameter_thresholds Numeric vector. Diameter cut-offs computing rootlength..* avg.diameter..* columns. Units set diameter_threshold_unit.  Default c(0.2, 0.5, 1). diameter_threshold_unit Character. Unit diameter_thresholds: "mm" (default), "cm", "px". output_path Character NULL. provided, result saved .RData file path.  exported object named root.depth.metrics.  Parent directories created exist.  Default NULL (file written). verbose Logical. Print per-image progress lines showing image index, per-image time, cumulative elapsed time, estimated remaining time, predicted clock-time completion.  Default TRUE.

title: Skeletonization Wrapper Function — skeletonize_image
text: img matrix, data frame, `SpatRaster` object representing binary image skeletonized. methods character vector specifying skeletonization methods apply. Valid options "ZhangSuen", "GuoHall", "MAT", "SteepestAscend". Defaults four methods. verbose Logical. TRUE, displays progress diagnostic messages processing. Defaults TRUE. select.layer Integer specifying layer use img multi-layer `SpatRaster`. Defaults NULL, may use package-specific defaults method.

title: Skeletonization Wrapper Function — skeletonize_image
text: single method selected, function returns `SpatRaster` object representing skeletonized image. multiple methods selected, named list `SpatRaster` objects returned, element named according method used.

title: Skeletonization Wrapper Function — skeletonize_image
text: function allows flexible streamlined skeletonization binary images using one supported algorithms: "ZhangSuen": Implements Zhang-Suen thinning algorithm, parallel iterative method preserves connectivity reducing binary objects skeletal representation. "GuoHall": Implements Guo-Hall thinning algorithm, improved parallel thinning method often produces cleaner skeletons better preservation shape characteristics. "MAT": Computes Medial Axis Transform extract skeleton based distance transform binary image. "SteepestAscend": Uses steepest ascent algorithm distance transform, foreground pixel traces path ridge points (skeleton) following steepest gradient. function processes input image specified methods returns results. multiple methods chosen, results returned named list, element corresponding method. method may different computational complexity produce slightly different skeletal representations.

title: Skeletonization Wrapper Function — skeletonize_image
text: Different skeletonization methods may produce varying results depending input image characteristics. Zhang-Suen Guo-Hall methods iterative thinning approaches, MAT SteepestAscend based distance transforms. Consider specific requirements analysis choosing methods.

title: Skeletonization Wrapper Function — skeletonize_image
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Load a binary image as a SpatRaster binary_image <- terra::rast(matrix(c(0, 1, 1, 0, 0, 1, 1, 0, 0), nrow = 3))  # Apply all skeletonization methods skeletons <- skeletonize_image(binary_image, verbose = TRUE)  # Apply only Zhang-Suen method zhang_skeleton <- skeletonize_image(binary_image, methods = "ZhangSuen")  # Apply only Zhang-Suen method SA_skeleton <- skeletonize_image(binary_image, methods = "SteepestAscend")  # Apply multiple specific methods selected_skeletons <- skeletonize_image(binary_image,                                         methods = c("ZhangSuen", "MAT"),                                        verbose = FALSE)  # Access results from multiple methods zhang_result <- selected_skeletons$ZhangSuen mat_result <- selected_skeletons$MAT } # }

text: function extracts zones image data based depth bins /rotation slices. supports three modes operation: "depth" Zones image pixels according binned depth values using provided depth map selected depth indices. "rotation" Zones image slicing according specified rotation slices along x-axis (rows). "" Applies depth zoning first, applies rotation slicing depth-zoned image.

title: Zone Image Data by Depth and/or Rotation Slices with Optional Spatial Cropping — zoning
text: terra::SpatRaster object containing zoned optionally cropped image data. Pixels matching zoning criteria set NA.

title: Zone Image Data by Depth and/or Rotation Slices with Optional Spatial Cropping — zoning
text: function supports flexible cropping input image using specified spatial extent, applied zoning operations. depth mode used, function warns cropping remove pixels containing desired depth values. function processes operations following order: Spatial cropping: crop_extent provided, img depth_map cropped first. Depth zoning: mode includes "depth", pixels masked based depth values depth_map. Rotation zoning: mode includes "rotation", image sliced along x-axis (rows) according rotation_slices. Layer selection: select_layer specified, layer retained. Depth Processing: depth single value, selects closest matching depth depth_map depth sequence (consecutive values), selects depths within range depth arbitrary vector, selects closest unique depths value Rotation Processing: Divides image rows rotation_total_slices conceptual slices Extracts rows corresponding slices rotation_slices[1] rotation_slices[2] Useful analyzing angular segments circular rotational patterns Performance Optimization: Uses vectorized operations terra::ifel() fast depth filtering Direct logical operations creating depth masks Eliminates intermediate raster creation better performance Warnings: function issues warnings : Cropping remove pixels containing desired depth values resulting image contains NA values

title: Zone Image Data by Depth and/or Rotation Slices with Optional Spatial Cropping — zoning
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Load example data data(seg_Oulanka2023_Session01_T067) img = terra::rast(seg_Oulanka2023_Session01_T067) depth_map = terra::t(create_depthmap(img))  # Depth zoning example - select depths 3 through 6 zone_img <- zone_image(   img = img,   mode = "depth",   depth_map = depth_map,   depth = 3:6 )  # Rotation zoning example - extract slices 2-4 out of 8 total zone_img <- zone_image(   img = img,   mode = "rotation",   rotation_slices = c(2, 4),   rotation_total_slices = 8 )  # Combined zoning with cropping zone_img <- zone_image(   img = img,   mode = "both",   depth_map = depth_map,   depth = c(4.5, 5.2, 6.0),   rotation_slices = c(1, 3),   rotation_total_slices = 5,   crop_extent = c(100, 500, 200, 400) )  # Select only the first layer and crop spatially zone_img <- zone_image(   img = img,   mode = "depth",   depth_map = depth_map,   depth = 5,   select_layer = 1,   crop_extent = c(0, 1000, 0, 1000) )  # Mixed depth selection: range plus individual values zone_img <- zone_image(   img = img,   mode = "depth",   depth_map = depth_map,   depth = c(3:8, 13, 15.5)  # Range 3-8 plus closest to 13 and 15.5 ) } # }
